scope: Pass B full-surface expansion, cross-checked against the real published OpenAPI spec (https://github.com/dbt-labs/dbt-cloud-openapi-spec/blob/master/openapi-v2.yaml, fetched 2026-07-04): every one of the 52 documented Administrative API v2 operations is enumerated below, each covered_by a stream/write or excluded with a specific reason. 10 streams (projects/runs/repositories/users/environments/jobs/invites/licenses/notifications/ssh_tunnels) and 13 writes (job CRUD + trigger/retry, run cancel/retry, notification CRUD, SSH tunnel CRUD) now cover every practical GET-list/detail resource and every dialect-expressible mutation. Excluded: Projects/Environments/Repositories v2 CRUD mutations (the v2 endpoints themselves are dbt-labs-deprecated in favor of v3, which this bundle's base_url/spec targets v2 only), Accounts/Permissions/Users mutation (account-admin scope, not syncable connector data), run/job artifact downloads (raw manifest.json/run_results.json/catalog.json file bodies, not a JSON list endpoint), and the notification-unsubscribe utility endpoint (a caller-identity action, not a data mutation on a record).

excluded.reason: dbt-labs-marked 'Deprecated. Consider using the v3 API instead' in the published OpenAPI spec; this bundle's account scoping uses the required account_id config value directly, never an account-listing call
account-level billing/plan metadata retrieval, not a syncable data stream; account_id is supplied directly via config, not discovered
mutates account-level billing/plan settings; account-admin scope, not connector-appropriate data mutation
partial account-level settings update; same account-admin scope as the full account update
single-SSH-tunnel detail is a strict subset of the ssh_tunnels stream's list response fields; the list stream already reads every tunnel
dbt-labs-marked 'Deprecated. Consider using the v3 API instead'; environment mutation belongs to the v3 Administrative API this connector does not target
single-environment detail is a strict subset of the environments stream's list response fields
dbt-labs-marked 'Deprecated. Consider using the v3 API instead'
dbt-labs-marked 'Deprecated. Consider using the v3 API instead'
single-invite detail is a strict subset of the invites stream's list response fields
single-job detail is a strict subset of the jobs stream's list response fields
downloads a specific completed run's manifest.json/run_results.json/catalog.json artifact file verbatim, not a structured JSON list/detail record
single-notification detail is a strict subset of the notifications stream's list response fields
mutates a user's license/role permission grant; account-admin identity-management action, not connector data
dbt-labs-marked 'Deprecated. Consider using the v3 API instead'
single-project detail is a strict subset of the projects stream's list response fields
dbt-labs-marked 'Deprecated. Consider using the v3 API instead'
dbt-labs-marked 'Deprecated. Consider using the v3 API instead'
dbt-labs-marked 'Deprecated. Consider using the v3 API instead'
single-repository detail is a strict subset of the repositories stream's list response fields
dbt-labs-marked 'Deprecated. Consider using the v3 API instead'
single-run detail is a strict subset of the runs stream's list response fields

lists artifact FILENAMES only as a precursor to downloading each raw artifact body; the artifacts themselves are binary_payload (see the per-file GET below), and the filename list carries no independently syncable record data
downloads a specific run's manifest.json/run_results.json/catalog.json artifact file verbatim, not a structured JSON list/detail record
retrieves retry-eligibility/failure-point details already present on the runs stream's own record (status/status_message); no independent record shape

a single run step's full stdout/debug log text is a large unbounded blob keyed by an id only discoverable by first reading a run's own step list (not itself a list endpoint dbt Cloud publishes); no practical stream shape without a parent-id fan_out this connector does not declare
single-user detail is a strict subset of the users stream's list response fields
mutates a user's own account profile fields; user-identity self-service action, not connector-appropriate data mutation
clears every email notification configuration for the CALLING user's own identity; a caller-identity action, not a mutation targeting a specific record the connector reads
